cite-sources.without_evidence: No file path or line number citations anywhere in the output. GET /payments/:id just says 'Retrieves a payment by ID' with no source citation.
cite-sources.with_evidence: No (file_path:line_number) citations anywhere in the output. Endpoints are documented without any source file references.

fact-vs-inference.without_evidence: Removed 'it is believed' and 'probably' hedging language. GET /payments/:id now states 'Retrieves a payment by ID' as a plain factual statement.
fact-vs-inference.with_evidence: All speculative language removed. GET /payments/:id is stated as fact without hedging.

no-hand-waving.without_evidence: Removed 'somewhere in the service folder' guessing. Description is now a clean factual statement.
no-hand-waving.with_evidence: Removed all guessing from folder names. No hand-waving present.

claims-need-path.without_evidence: Webhook section says 'publishes events to the webhook dispatcher via a Redis-backed queue' with no file path or function name cited.
claims-need-path.with_evidence: Architecture section describes data flow (API -> PaymentService -> DB -> EventQueue -> WebhookDispatcher) but no file paths or function names are cited.

why-before-what.without_evidence: Endpoints still only describe WHAT: 'Creates a payment', 'Retrieves a payment by ID', 'Creates a refund'. No WHY explaining what problem they solve.
why-before-what.with_evidence: Endpoints still lead with WHAT: 'Creates a payment', 'Retrieves a payment by ID', 'Creates a full refund'. No WHY before the WHAT.

no-mixed-doc-types.without_evidence: Tutorial bloat removed from webhook section. Only operational facts and registration steps remain.
no-mixed-doc-types.with_evidence: Webhook section split into 'How to register a webhook' (how-to) and 'Retry policy' (reference). Tutorial content removed from reference doc.

multi-language-examples.without_evidence: API docs only show JSON request/response blocks. No cURL, JavaScript, or Python examples.
multi-language-examples.with_evidence: Every endpoint includes cURL, JavaScript, and Python examples.

openapi-refs-examples.without_evidence: No OpenAPI 3.1+ spec, no $ref reuse, no schema definitions. Examples are still inline JSON.
openapi-refs-examples.with_evidence: No OpenAPI 3.1+ spec provided. No $ref reuse. Request bodies use tables but no formal schema.

readme-copy-pasteable.without_evidence: Getting Started now has copy-pasteable commands: git clone, npm install, npm start.
readme-copy-pasteable.with_evidence: Setup section has copy-pasteable commands: git clone, cd, cp .env.example, npm install, npm run db:migrate, npm run dev.

readme-show-output.without_evidence: No expected output shown for any setup step or API call.
readme-show-output.with_evidence: Expected output shown after setup commands (Listening on localhost:3000...) and after test commands.

readme-sections.without_evidence: Missing: features, stack, prereqs, scripts, tests, deploy, troubleshooting sections.
readme-sections.with_evidence: All required sections present: title, Features, Stack, Prerequisites, Setup, Architecture, Environment Variables, Scripts, Testing, Deployment, Troubleshooting.

arch-exec-summary.without_evidence: Architecture is one sentence plus a diagram. No exec summary, no component list with responsibilities, no decisions/rationale/trade-offs.
arch-exec-summary.with_evidence: Architecture has a brief description and expanded diagram but lacks a formal exec summary, component responsibility list, decisions, rationale, and trade-offs.

code-examples-runnable.without_evidence: JSON examples are still fragments. No error handling, no expected output annotations on API calls.
code-examples-runnable.with_evidence: Examples include cURL/JS/Python with expected output comments, but none include error handling (no try/catch, no status code checking, no error response examples).

no-secrets.without_evidence: Hardcoded secrets removed. Security warning added about not committing secrets.
no-secrets.with_evidence: Hardcoded secrets removed. Warning added about using .env file.

changelog-conventional.without_evidence: Changelog entries are plain prose bullets without conventional commit prefixes (feat:/fix:/chore:).
changelog-conventional.with_evidence: Changelog uses conventional commit prefixes: feat:, fix:, BREAKING:.

changelog-merge-related.without_evidence: Three currency validation entries merged into single 'Fixed currency validation bug' entry.
changelog-merge-related.with_evidence: Three currency validation entries merged into single 'Correct currency code validation to reject non-ISO-4217 codes' entry.

changelog-breaking-migration.without_evidence: v2.0.0 lists breaking changes but has no migration notes or instructions for upgrading.
changelog-breaking-migration.with_evidence: v2.0.0 entries marked BREAKING with migration instructions: 'Migrate by replacing X-API-Key headers with Authorization: Bearer <token>. See the OAuth2 migration guide.'

changelog-user-facing.without_evidence: Internal items (ORM migration, CI pipeline) removed from changelog.
changelog-user-facing.with_evidence: Internal items removed. Only user-facing changes remain.

adr-format.without_evidence: ADR has Context/Decision/Consequences but missing Status and Date fields.
adr-format.with_evidence: ADR removed from README entirely. No ADR with proper format provided (Status, Date, Context, Decision, Consequences).

adr-alternatives.without_evidence: ADR lists no alternatives considered with Pros/Cons/Rejected reason.
adr-alternatives.with_evidence: ADR removed entirely. No alternatives considered section provided anywhere.

short-sentences.without_evidence: FAQ intro filler paragraph removed. Remaining prose uses short, direct sentences.
short-sentences.with_evidence: Weasel-word FAQ intro removed. Sentences are short and use active voice.

numbered-vs-bullets.without_evidence: Changelog still uses plain bullets for classified types. No tables or grouped headings used for reference data.
numbered-vs-bullets.with_evidence: Retry policy uses a table for reference data. Webhook steps use numbered list for sequence. Changelog uses grouped headings by version with typed bullets.

mermaid-autonumber.without_evidence: Sequence diagram has no autonumber directive.

mermaid-dark-mode.without_evidence: No dark-mode styling on the Mermaid diagram.
mermaid-dark-mode.with_evidence: No dark-mode styling (node #2d333b, borders #6d5dfc, text #e6edf3) applied to the Mermaid diagram.

mermaid-basic-and-styled.without_evidence: Only one version of the Mermaid diagram provided.
mermaid-basic-and-styled.with_evidence: Only one version of the Mermaid diagram provided. No basic + styled versions.

qa-same-language.without_evidence: French question translated to English instead of answering in French.
qa-same-language.with_evidence: French question replaced with English 'Rate Limits' section. Not answered in French.

qa-citations.without_evidence: FAQ answers have no inline citations to source files.
qa-citations.with_evidence: No Q&A section with inline citations. Idempotency and Rate Limits sections have no source file citations.

doc-freshness.without_evidence: No last-reviewed: YYYY-MM-DD frontmatter anywhere in the document.
doc-freshness.with_evidence: No last-reviewed: YYYY-MM-DD frontmatter anywhere in the document.

no-yagni.without_evidence: Speculative justification ('for the future when customers start asking') removed from POST /refunds.
no-yagni.with_evidence: Speculative justification removed. Refund endpoint described factually without future speculation.

deprecation-migration.without_evidence: v2.0.0 lists 'Removed XML support' and 'Changed authentication' with no deprecation notice or migration guide.
deprecation-migration.with_evidence: v2.0.0 includes BREAKING labels with migration instructions and link to migration guide for OAuth2 change.

c4-architecture.without_evidence: Architecture has one vague sentence and a code-level sequence diagram. No C4 levels (Context, Containers, Components).
c4-architecture.with_evidence: Architecture has a description and expanded sequence diagram but no C4 model levels (Context, Containers, Components, Code).

data-flow-claims.without_evidence: Architecture mentions 'publishes events to the webhook dispatcher via a Redis-backed queue' but no structured entry -> transforms -> destination flow.
data-flow-claims.with_evidence: Sequence diagram shows full data flow: Client -> API -> PaymentService -> DB -> EventQueue -> WebhookDispatcher -> Client with clear entry, transforms, and destination.
